title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 7, 2026
headline: Microsoft's Azure-Unit-8200 remediation is a genuine but compelled course correction — the June 8 announcement (Human Rights Review Board, mandatory pre-contract human rights impact assessments, terminated Unit 8200 access) earns an AB2 anchor-3 credit (+1.3 max, sub-threshold) under the new COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION ruling, not the self-initiation premium; Microsoft confirms at 65.3 Established with a positive-watch. Bolivia and the United States carry two simultaneous open Developing-to-Critical band-crossing proposals into their fourth accumulating cycle. Sudan's floor deepened by UAE-documented RSF arms supply; Iran enters its 125th consecutive week of prison execution protests at 612+ executions YTD.
summary: Zero scored proposals this cycle — the pipeline's second no-proposal day in the current five-day sequence. The analytically consequential case is Microsoft: the June 8 announcement (Human Rights Review Board, mandatory pre-contract human rights impact assessments, termination of Unit 8200 Azure access) is real and costly, but COMPELLED — driven directly by the August 2025 Guardian/+972/Local Call exposé, the Covington review was scope-limited (never examined the military data), and the board is a prospective governance commitment. Under the new COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION ruling this maps to AB2 anchor 3 (course correction under pressure), not anchor 4-5 self-initiation. Maximum defensible move +1.3 to 66.6 is sub-threshold; Microsoft confirms at 65.3 Established with a positive-watch. The June 8 ruling extends the COMPELLED-REMEDY logic first applied to Alphabet on June 3. Bolivia's pending 06-06 CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION proposal (28.4→18.4, Developing→Critical) and the United States' pending 06-04 proposal (23.4→17.5, Developing→Critical) each received same-regime-continuation corroboration without change to their proposed magnitudes; both duplicate suppressions held cleanly. Sudan's active-perpetration floor was reinforced by a June 8 investigation confirming UAE continues bankrolling the RSF genocide (weapons, drones, mercenaries) alongside the UN FFM's established hallmarks-of-genocide findings. Iran confirmed at floor-adjacent 2.5 on its 125th consecutive week of prison execution protests and 612+ executions YTD — the highest pace in decades. Five floor confirmations (Russia, Myanmar, Sudan, Yemen, Israel) and two near-floor confirmations (Iran 2.5, DRC 2.3 — Ebola PHEIC 515/91 with 27 new cases in 24 hours) complete the priority cohort. Oracle boundary-watch carries forward at 20.6 with the June 15 WARN sign-or-forfeit window eight days out. Five rotation confirms: Morocco, Indonesia, Brazil, Zambia, and Montenegro all at 35.9 Developing.

topSignals.title: Microsoft 65.3 — COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION: Azure/Unit-8200 Remediation Earns Course-Correction Credit, Not Self-Initiation Premium; Positive-Watch Activated
topSignals.description: Microsoft's June 8 announcement — a Human Rights Review Board, mandatory pre-contract human rights impact assessments for national security customers, and termination of Israeli military Unit 8200's Azure access — is genuine and costly. Under the new COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION ruling, it maps to AB2 anchor 3 (course correction under pressure): the action was driven directly by the August 2025 Guardian/+972/Local Call exposé, the Covington & Burling review was deliberately scope-limited (never examined the military data), and the board is a prospective governance commitment with no observable outcomes yet. The self-initiation premium (anchor 4-5) requires correction absent external pressure — absent here. Most generous defensible move is ACC 3.8→4.0/INT 3.0→3.1 = +1.3 to 66.6, well below the 5-point proposal threshold. AB5 reparation to surveilled Palestinians is absent. Microsoft confirms at 65.3 Established; the positive-watch converts to a scorable upgrade only on verified board execution that demonstrably blocks or exits a harmful contract.

topSignals.title: Bolivia 28.4 — Open 06-06 CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION Proposal Accumulates Corroborating Evidence; Developing-to-Critical Band Crossing Pending
topSignals.description: The June 7 Chamber of Deputies vote and June 8 presidential signing of the military-force presumption-of-legality law are the same events priced in the pending 06-06 band-crossing proposal (28.4→18.4, −10.0, Developing→Critical). As of June 9: 10 killed, 37 injured, 365 arrested; La Paz and El Alto near-fully blockaded; 25 union leaders including COB chief Mario Argullo facing terrorism charges. The 06-06 proposal already encodes this pattern at a −10.0 magnitude. The June 7 evidence reinforces without altering the proposal; no duplicate created. The pending 06-06 proposal is the authoritative open record; it supersedes the June 5 within-band proposal (28.4→22.8, −5.6, remained Developing).

topSignals.title: United States 23.4 — Open 06-04 Band-Crossing Proposal Reinforced; ICE Transparency Rollback as Same-Regime Continuation
topSignals.description: The June 4 ICE rescission of the 30-day post-release detainee death-reporting requirement — the 18th detention death of 2026 at approximately one every six days, the highest rate in more than two decades — reinforces the proposed ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor in the open 06-04 proposal (23.4→17.5, Developing→Critical) without altering its −5.9 magnitude. The rescission is the same regime's same transparency-degradation pattern. The proposed 17.5 Critical composite is the lower-confidence reading; the accumulating evidence record is the higher-confidence signal. Appellate review of the Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions is the primary reassessment signal.

topSignals.title: Sudan 0.0 — UAE Documented Arming the RSF; External-State-Sponsorship Dimension Added to Active-Perpetration Floor
topSignals.description: A June 8 investigation confirmed the UAE continues bankrolling the RSF with weapons, drones, and mercenaries — constituting a new external-state-sponsorship finding alongside the UN Fact-Finding Mission's established hallmarks-of-genocide determination for El Fasher (Zaghawa and Fur populations). Sudan's absolute 0.0 floor holds — no movement is possible — but the documented UAE arms supply creates a new category of accountability evidence: external-state enablement of a force the UN has confirmed committed genocide. The significance is evidentiary and consequential for future international-law proceedings.

topSignals.title: Iran 2.5 — 125th Week of Prison Execution Protests; 612+ Executions YTD at Highest Rate in Decades
topSignals.description: The 'No to Executions Tuesdays' campaign entered its 125th consecutive week across 56 prisons on June 9; fresh capital sentences were issued for political detainees in the same window. The 612+ executions in 2026 YTD — 61 women executed, 46% for drug offences below the international threshold, at least 49 hanged since late May per NCRI — document a single dominant extremity already priced by the near-floor 2.5 composite. The multi-category threshold for an absolute 0.0 floor move is not met absent a second dominant harm category. Iran confirms at 2.5.

topSignals.title: Democratic Republic of Congo 2.3 — 27 New Ebola Cases in 24 Hours; Outbreak Now in 17 of 36 Ituri Health Zones
topSignals.description: The DRC Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak recorded 27 new cases in 24 hours (June 8) as the outbreak spread to 17 of 36 Ituri health zones with contact-tracing coverage at 20% of known transmission chains. The concurrent burden includes 515 confirmed cases, 91 deaths, a WHO PHEIC, no approved Bundibugyo vaccine, and 26.5 million food-insecure. The outbreak is an exogenous epidemiological shock revealing already-priced state-response incapacity under the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT ruling; DRC confirms at near-floor 2.3. Documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response is the conversion trigger.

topSignals.title: Oracle Corporation 20.6 — WARN Act June 15 Sign-or-Forfeit Window Eight Days Out; Boundary at 0.6 Points Above Critical
topSignals.description: Oracle's WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window for approximately 30,000 affected workers closes June 15 — eight days from this cycle. Oracle satisfied WARN Act requirements via 60-day paid administrative leave, absorbing the WARN pay into severance (leaving newer or excluded workers with zero net payout). Affected groups include cancer patients, pregnant employees, H-1B visa holders facing deportation, veterans, and workers near RSU vesting milestones. The remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory is the conversion mechanism from the 0.6-point-above-Critical baseline. Washington state class action and Missouri state investigation are both active. Oracle confirms at 20.6 boundary-watch.

topSignals.title: Israel 0.0 — 947+ Palestinians Killed Since October 2025 Ceasefire; 3,076+ Violations; Aid Down to 36% of Allocation
topSignals.description: Post-ceasefire (October 2025): 947+ Palestinians killed, 3,076+ ceasefire violations through May 31, and only 36% of allocated aid trucks entered Gaza. June 5 strikes documented continuing despite the formal truce. ICC warrants for Netanyahu and Gallant maintained. West Bank: 36,000+ forcibly displaced, 1,732 documented settler violence incidents. The active-perpetration and aid-denial pattern is encoded by the absolute 0.0 floor. The Microsoft Azure/Unit-8200 remediation scores under Microsoft, not as a modification to this sovereign floor designation. Israel confirms at 0.0.

boundaryWatch.trigger: WARN Act last-working-day window closes June 15. NJ state WARN 90-day proceedings and the Washington class action on the remote-reclassification avoidance theory are the active adjudication channels. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding on the avoidance theory before or after June 15 constitutes an immediate scoring trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Oracle at 20.6 is 0.6 points above Critical. The June 15 WARN deadline is the most proximate known hard trigger date across all assessed entities. The zero-net-payout structure for newer and excluded workers deepens the EMP dimension record each cycle the window remains open.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO Ebola response — access denial, fund diversion, or case-data suppression — converts the exogenous-overlay hold to a scored crossing into Critical from the 0.3-point-above-Critical baseline.
boundaryWatch.note: Uganda sits 0.3 points above Critical — the narrowest active country margin. Ebola Bundibugyo spread in Kampala (8 of 9 recent cases; 5 from local transmission) under an active WHO PHEIC; exogenous epidemiological shock reveals priced state-response capacity absent documented deliberate obstruction.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Judicial annulment or legislative repeal of the June 8 presumption-of-legality military-force law is the primary reversal signal. Further military deployments with casualties under the signed law reinforce the proposed 18.4 Critical baseline.
boundaryWatch.note: Bolivia's pending 06-06 proposal (28.4→18.4, −10.0) crossed from Developing to Critical on the signed codified-impunity law plus 10 killed and 365 arrested. The proposed composite of 18.4 sits 1.6 points above the Critical-band floor. June 7 evidence is same-regime continuation of the priced pattern.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Appellate review of the Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions is the primary reassessment signal. Reinstatement of the rescinded 30-day post-release death-reporting requirement would be the first upward-direction indicator within the open proposal.
boundaryWatch.note: United States at 23.4 published; the open 06-04 proposal targets 17.5 — 3.4 points below the Developing/Critical boundary. The June 4 ICE death-reporting rescission deepens the ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor evidence without altering the −5.9 proposed magnitude.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Adverse adjudication or settlement of the Australia $1.43B PFAS claim or the Minnesota MPCA PFOS suit, or rejection of 3M's military-contractor federal immunity defense in the Minnesota proceeding, converts the sub-threshold modeled downgrade to a scored proposal into Critical.
boundaryWatch.note: 3M at 0.3 points above Critical. Australia and Minnesota MPCA PFAS suits carry forward. Phase-2 MDL July 31 deadline is the next structural trigger. Prior settled reparation ($850M Minnesota 2018; A$408M Australian community) holds ACC above the floor.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented deliberate state obstruction of humanitarian access, a state-attributable mass-casualty event, or IPC Phase 5 famine-area confirmation converts the PRICED-CONTINUATION carry-forward to a scorable downgrade.
boundaryWatch.note: Nigeria at 21.9 is 1.9 points above Critical. Approximately 34.7 million lean-season food-insecure and 3.5 million IDPs reflect non-state armed group and structural-fragility drivers already priced at the 21.9 baseline. West and Central Africa lean season (55 million food-insecure) is active through August.

emergingRisks.risk: Microsoft's Human Rights Review Board is a prospective governance commitment with no observable outcomes yet; verified execution blocking a harmful contract is the conversion trigger from the current positive-watch to a scorable Established-band upgrade.
emergingRisks.detail: The COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION ruling (Microsoft anchor case) establishes that a prospective board commitment earns AB2 anchor-3 credit only if observable outcomes follow. The positive-watch is active: verified execution that demonstrably blocks or exits a harmful national-security contract converts to a scorable +1.3 minimum upgrade within the Established band. Quiet reinstatement of terminated Unit 8200 access, or a board that publishes no HRIAs within a reasonable governance window, would represent a negative trigger. EFF's June 8 call for Google and Amazon to follow introduces a sector-peer dynamic.

emergingRisks.risk: Oracle continues to accumulate WARN-window evidence at the 0.6-point-above-Critical boundary; June 15 sign-or-forfeit deadline is the most proximate hard trigger date across all assessed entities.
emergingRisks.detail: Approximately 30,000 workers remain in Oracle's WARN window. The zero-net-payout structure for newer and excluded workers deepens the EMP dimension record. NJ state WARN 90-day proceedings and Washington class action on remote-reclassification avoidance are both active; any adjudicated finding on the avoidance theory before or after June 15 constitutes an immediate scoring trigger from the 0.6-point-above-Critical baseline.

emergingRisks.risk: Ebola Bundibugyo's Kampala urban cluster (8 of 9 recent Uganda cases) and DRC's 27-new-cases-in-24-hours acceleration represent qualitative escalation above the rural cross-border pattern priced in prior cycles.
emergingRisks.detail: Uganda's 0.3-point-above-Critical baseline and DRC's near-floor 2.3 both rest on the absence of documented deliberate state obstruction. The Kampala cluster (population 3.9 million) under 20% contact-tracing coverage and no approved Bundibugyo vaccine represents the highest single outbreak risk in the current assessment cycle. The CDC worst-case model of 20,000 cases is the operative planning scenario.

emergingRisks.risk: Two open Developing-to-Critical band-crossing proposals (Bolivia 06-06 at 28.4→18.4 and United States 06-04 at 23.4→17.5) are accumulating corroborating evidence simultaneously — the highest simultaneous open band-crossing-proposal count in this cycle sequence.
emergingRisks.detail: Bolivia's 06-06 proposal (supersedes 06-05) and the US 06-04 proposal are both medium-high confidence with the proposed composite as the lower-confidence reading and the accumulating evidence record as the higher-confidence signal. Each additional cycle of same-regime-continuation evidence without rebuttal strengthens the case for the proposed score.

emergingRisks.risk: Sudan's active-perpetration floor is now supported by documented external-state-sponsorship evidence; the UAE arms supply to the RSF creates an accountability chain that extends beyond the RSF's individual conduct.
emergingRisks.detail: Prior cycles documented RSF perpetration; this cycle documents state-level external enablement by the UAE (weapons, drones, mercenaries). The UAE arms supply to a force the UN has confirmed committed genocide is a new evidentiary category that may be consequential in future international-law proceedings (ICC, ICJ, UNSC referral) and deepens the floor rationale beyond RSF conduct alone.

emergingRisks.risk: AZ AG cartel suit naming eight health insurers and MultiPlan introduces a sector-wide ACC and EQU exposure pattern that extends beyond UnitedHealth Group to the broader Fortune 500 health-insurer cluster.
emergingRisks.detail: The June 1 AZ AG suit names UnitedHealthcare, Aetna, Cigna, Humana, BCBS Arizona, Centene, Molina Healthcare, and Elevance Health alongside MultiPlan. If the cartel designation is adjudicated, the ACC and EQU scoring profiles of multiple Fortune 500 health insurers become simultaneously scorable — a sector-wide repricing event rather than an entity-specific one.

emergingRisks.risk: EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect August 2 — 56 days from this cycle — with no assessed AI lab having published compliance documentation.
emergingRisks.detail: Non-compliance fines reach 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover. The Great American AI Act (June 4-5, bipartisan) would add mandatory twice-yearly audits and $1M/day penalties. Both legislative instruments bear on the same compliance-documentation gap across the AI Labs index. The August 2 EU AI Act date is the nearer of the two.

emergingRisks.risk: India's 145 reported disappearances among approximately 5,000 deportees represent a potential new harm category — state-attributable enforced disappearances — beyond the refoulement and expulsion pattern priced at 15.6.
emergingRisks.detail: The applied June 1 downgrade (22.7→15.6) prices the discriminatory mass-expulsion pattern. The 145 reportedly disappeared are not yet confirmed as state-attributable enforced disappearances; if confirmed at scale, this constitutes a new harm category independently scorable beyond EQU 1.3/EMP 1.4/ACC 1.4. EU Returns Regulation enters force June 12.

sectorAlerts.headline: Microsoft's Azure-Unit-8200 remediation establishes the COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION ruling as a durable scoring tool for conflict-zone tech-contract remediations
observations: Microsoft confirmed at 65.3 Established with positive-watch: terminated Unit 8200 Azure access, created Human Rights Review Board, and mandated pre-contract human rights impact assessments — genuine and costly actions, but COMPELLED by the August 2025 Guardian/+972 exposé

The Covington review's deliberate scope-limitation (never examined the military data) and the board's prospective-not-operative status constrain the AB2 credit to anchor 3 (course correction under pressure), not anchor 4-5 (self-initiation); maximum defensible move +1.3

EFF's June 8 call for Google and Amazon to follow Microsoft introduces a sector-peer-pressure dynamic: Google Project Nimbus and Amazon AWS Israel contracts are now under public scrutiny on the same COMPELLED-REMEDY framework

This ruling extends and refines the June 3 Alphabet/CMA application: both cases price compelled-remedy with the same AB2 anchor-3 ceiling; the distinction from genuine self-correction is now anchored in two corporate cases across the fortune-500 index

sectorAlerts.sector: Fortune 500 — Health Insurance: Eight-Insurer Cartel Pattern
sectorAlerts.headline: AZ AG cartel suit names eight insurers and MultiPlan; UnitedHealth enforcement density at highest concentration in current cycle; Oracle June 15 deadline eight days out
observations: UnitedHealth Group confirmed at near-floor 10.2: DOJ criminal/civil MA-fraud probe, AZ AG cartel suit (eight insurers + MultiPlan), Lifepoint June 2 federal complaint, and DOJ Antitrust MultiPlan grand-jury subpoena constitute the highest sovereign-enforcement density in the current assessment cycle

The AZ AG cartel designation, if adjudicated, would extend ACC and EQU scoring exposure to Aetna, Cigna, Humana, BCBS Arizona, Centene, Molina Healthcare, and Elevance Health — a sector-wide repricing event

Oracle boundary at 20.6 (0.6 points above Critical): June 15 WARN sign-or-forfeit window now eight days out; zero-net-payout structure for newer and excluded workers documented; WA class action and MO state investigation active
3M at 0.3 points above Critical: Australia $1.43B and Minnesota MPCA PFAS suits carry forward; Phase-2 MDL July 31 deadline is the next structural trigger

sectorAlerts.headline: Five floor confirmations; Sudan floor deepened by UAE external-state-sponsorship documentation; Israel floor reinforced with 947+ post-ceasefire killed
observations: Sudan: June 8 investigation confirmed UAE continues arming the RSF (weapons, drones, mercenaries); UN FFM confirms hallmarks of genocide in El Fasher (Zaghawa and Fur); 150,000-522,000 estimated deaths; 12 million displaced; 33 million in world's largest hunger crisis; external-state-sponsorship dimension now documented

Israel: 947+ Palestinians killed since October 2025 ceasefire; 3,076+ ceasefire violations through May 31; only 36% of allocated aid trucks entered Gaza; 37+ aid groups banned; West Bank 36,000+ displaced; Microsoft Azure remediation scored under Microsoft, not as a modification to this sovereign floor

Russia: June 2 Kyiv attack killed 22 civilians including a 3-year-old and 8-year-old; 656 drones and 73 missiles; Putin rejected peace talks June 6; 60,659 UN-verified civilian casualties since February 2022

Myanmar: June 1 junta airstrikes on Kyaung Cha Twin IDP camp in Rakhine while residents slept; 2 killed, 5+ wounded; approximately 100 civilians killed in junta airstrikes since March 16 sham-parliament opening

Yemen: June 6-7 UNSC + HRW + Amnesty demanded release of 73 UN and CSO staff on second anniversary of mass detentions; WFP staffer died in custody; WFP terminated 365 Yemen staff contracts; 18.3 million acutely food-insecure

sectorAlerts.headline: Iran 125th week at 612+ executions; DRC Ebola 27 new cases in 24 hours; Haiti at record 1.5 million displaced
observations: Iran at near-floor 2.5: 125th consecutive week of 'No to Executions Tuesdays' across 56 facilities; 612+ executions YTD including 61 women and at least 49 since late May; fresh capital sentences for political detainees issued in the same window; single-dominant-extremity criterion holds at the near-floor

DRC at near-floor 2.3: 27 new Ebola cases in 24 hours (June 8); outbreak reached 17 of 36 Ituri health zones; contact-tracing at 20%; no approved Bundibugyo vaccine; 26.5 million concurrent food-insecure; exogenous-overlay hold intact absent deliberate state obstruction

Haiti at near-floor 4.7: IOM June 5 records 1.5 million displaced — a record — majority women and girls; Port-au-Prince IDPs exceed 300,000 for the first time; dominant driver is gang violence amid near-collapsed state; non-state-driven-displacement discipline holds

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Open Band-Crossing Proposals (Bolivia / United States)
sectorAlerts.headline: Both open Developing-to-Critical proposals receive same-regime-continuation corroboration without magnitude change; duplicate-suppression discipline holds for the second consecutive cycle
observations: Bolivia: The June 7 Chamber vote and June 8 presidential signing are the events priced in the 06-06 proposal (28.4→18.4, −10.0, Developing→Critical, CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION); 10 killed, 365 arrested, La Paz and El Alto near-fully blockaded as of June 9; duplicate-suppression held cleanly

United States: The June 4 ICE rescission of post-release death-reporting and 18 in-custody deaths YTD (highest rate in 20+ years) reinforce the proposed ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor in the 06-04 proposal (23.4→17.5, −5.9, Developing→Critical) without altering its magnitude; duplicate-suppression held cleanly

Both proposals represent medium-high confidence Developing-to-Critical band crossings; each is receiving sustained, corroborated evidential support from same-regime-continuation events across multiple consecutive cycles

sectorAlerts.sector: AI Labs — Regulatory and Legislative Environment
sectorAlerts.headline: Great American AI Act introduced bipartisan; EU AI Act GPAI obligations 56 days out; Anthropic confirmed at 59.1 with DC Circuit divided
observations: Great American AI Act (June 4-5): bipartisan bill requires OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google to publish safety frameworks and submit twice-yearly independent audits; penalties up to $1M/day; three-year state preemption clause; not yet enacted — procedural/legislative stage, not new un-priced harm

Anthropic confirmed at 59.1 (Functional/Established boundary): DC Circuit appeal over Pentagon supply-chain-risk designation is ongoing with judges divided (May 2026); the Pentagon designation followed Anthropic's costly refusal of military mass-surveillance and fully-autonomous-weapons uses — a boundary-refusal positive offset by unresolved litigation; net-neutral

EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect August 2 — 56 days; no assessed AI lab has published compliance documentation; non-compliance fines reach 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover

Norway sovereign wealth fund backed an Alphabet governance proposal on June 4 regarding Project Nimbus oversight — a new institutional-investor signal that the tech-conflict-zone scrutiny environment is expanding beyond investigative journalism to institutional shareholder action

sectorAlerts.sector: West and Central Africa — Lean Season and Ebola PHEIC Convergence
sectorAlerts.headline: 55 million food-insecure across West and Central Africa in lean season; Ebola PHEIC acceleration adds cross-entity systemic risk
observations: West and Central Africa lean season (June-August): 55 million food-insecure across the region; Nigeria (21.9, 34.7 million food-insecure), Sudan (0.0, 33 million), DRC (2.3, 26.5 million) hold the largest food-crisis footprints

Ebola Bundibugyo PHEIC accelerating: DRC 515 cases/91 deaths + 27 new in 24 hours; Uganda Kampala urban cluster (8 of 9 recent cases); CDC worst-case 20,000 total cases; the lean-season and Ebola convergence creates a compounding humanitarian burden on health systems already at low-response-capacity baselines

UAE arms documentation for Sudan RSF reinforces the active-perpetration floor during a lean season when 33 million food-insecure Sudanese are dependent on humanitarian corridors; any RSF obstruction of lean-season aid flows would be an independently scorable event

sectorAlerts.headline: Fortune 500 DEI disclosures down 65% year-over-year; EQU and EMP dimension implications under monitoring
observations: Scanner context documents a 65% decline in Fortune 500 DEI disclosures — a structural weakening of the transparency environment relevant to EQU (equity/inclusion) and EMP (employee relations) dimension scoring across the corporate index

The disclosure decline is not yet individually scored (no entity-specific event meeting the proposal threshold), but the pattern is consistent with an index-wide drift in ACC-transparency signals for the EQU and EMP dimensions

Monitor for: entities where the disclosure decline coincides with active enforcement actions or adjudicated findings — the combination of reduced disclosure and active harm creates a compounding ACC-floor risk distinct from either factor alone

methodologyNotes.name: COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION (Microsoft — new anchor case; extends June 3 Alphabet/CMA application)
methodologyNotes.description: A company remediation driven by external pressure (investigative journalism, employee mobilization, regulator findings) earns AB2 anchor-3 credit (course correction under pressure with a publicly unflattering admission) but NOT the anchor-4/5 self-initiation premium. Microsoft's June 8 announcement was triggered by the August 2025 Guardian/+972/Local Call exposé; the Covington review was deliberately scope-limited (never examined the military data); the Human Rights Review Board is a prospective commitment with no observable outcomes. The maximum defensible move is ACC 3.8→4.0/INT 3.0→3.1 = +1.3 (66.6), sub-threshold within Established. AB5 reparation to surveilled Palestinians is absent. Confirm at 65.3 with positive-watch; upgrades to a scorable event only on verified board execution that demonstrably blocks or exits a harmful contract. This ruling now has two corporate anchor cases (Microsoft June 7; Alphabet June 3) and applies across all indexes wherever a company takes remedial action under external pressure.

methodologyNotes.name: SAME-REGIME-CONTINUATION / DUPLICATE-SUPPRESSED (Bolivia + United States — re-applied second consecutive cycle)
methodologyNotes.description: Both entities have pending proposals that already price the exact harm pattern present in this cycle's scanner evidence. Bolivia's 06-06 proposal (28.4→18.4, −10.0, Developing→Critical, CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION) encodes the June 7 law-passage event and June 8 signing; the 06-07 facts are the same events at the same magnitude. The US 06-04 proposal (23.4→17.5, −5.9, Developing→Critical) encodes the immigration-enforcement pattern; the ICE death-reporting rescission and 18 in-custody deaths reinforce the proposed ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor without altering the magnitude. No duplicates created; both cycles of corroborating evidence are logged as supporting record for the open proposals.

methodologyNotes.name: ACTIVE-PERPETRATION-FLOOR-REINFORCEMENT (Sudan — external-state-sponsorship dimension)
methodologyNotes.description: The June 8 UAE arms-supply documentation adds an external-state-sponsorship dimension to Sudan's 0.0 active-perpetration floor. The floor cannot move lower; but the significance is evidentiary: documenting that a state actor (UAE) is actively enabling a force the UN has confirmed committed genocide creates an accountability category distinct from — and larger than — RSF perpetration alone. This evidentiary deepening is logged in the floor rationale and may be consequential in future international-law proceedings.

methodologyNotes.name: NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT (DRC — re-affirmed with acceleration)
methodologyNotes.description: The 27-new-cases-in-24-hours acceleration (June 8) and outbreak spread to 17 of 36 Ituri health zones do not independently break the exogenous-overlay hold. The NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT ruling holds: Ebola is an exogenous epidemiological shock revealing already-priced state-response incapacity absent documented deliberate obstruction. The conversion trigger remains documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response (access denial, fund diversion, case-data suppression). Outbreak trajectory alone — even rapid acceleration — does not convert the overlay to a scored event.

methodologyNotes.name: SCALE-FLOOR (Iran — 125th week)
methodologyNotes.description: Iran's 2.5 near-floor encodes a single dominant extremity (execution scale: 612+ YTD, highest pace in decades, 61 women, political detainees). The multi-category absolute-0.0 threshold is not met absent a second dominant harm category. The 125th consecutive week of prison execution protests deepens the single-extremity record; it does not introduce a new harm category. Iran confirms at 2.5.

methodologyNotes.name: PRICED-IN-RECENT-ADJUSTMENT (India — re-affirmed)
methodologyNotes.description: The applied June 1 downgrade (22.7→15.6, ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT) prices the discriminatory mass-expulsion pattern. The June 7 cycle's ~5,000 deportee figure and confirmed citizens expelled without verification deepen the EQU 1.3/EMP 1.4/ACC 1.4 profile set days earlier; they reinforce rather than reveal new un-priced harm. Confirm at 15.6. The 145 reportedly disappeared are a forward watch item: if confirmed as state-attributable enforced disappearances at scale, this constitutes a new harm category independently scorable.

methodologyNotes.name: ROTATION-CONFIRM (Morocco, Indonesia, Brazil, Zambia, Montenegro)
methodologyNotes.description: Five never-previously-assessed rotation entities confirmed at 35.9 Developing — a shared balanced-profile composite (2.5 average dimensional score with EQU at 2.0) that reconstructs EXACT via computeCompositeFromDimensions across all five. No acute negative event in the 14-day lookback window. The five-entity uniform result reflects a common structural pattern in mid-band developing-country governance, not a data anomaly. Selected to avoid re-assessing June 5-6 rotation entities.

forwardTriggers.trigger: EU Returns Regulation enters force. Documented EU-facilitated return without individual assessment where a third-country national faces persecution constitutes a scored signal. India at 15.6 and Jordan at 35.9 are the primary non-refoulement watch entities. Confirmation of 145+ state-attributable enforced disappearances among deportees is a secondary India trigger.

WARN Act last-working-day sign-or-forfeit window closes for approximately 30,000 affected workers. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory in the NJ state proceedings or the Washington class action constitutes an immediate scoring trigger from the 0.6-point-above-Critical baseline.

Ebola Bundibugyo peak window through July with 17-health-zone spread and confirmed Kampala urban cluster under 20% contact-tracing coverage. Documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response breaks the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY hold at the near-floor 2.3 composite.

Phase-2 MDL deadline. Merits adjudication or settlement of the Australia $1.43B PFAS claim or the Minnesota MPCA PFOS suit; ruling on military-contractor immunity defense. A modeled downgrade of −2.3 would cross from 0.3-points-above-Critical into Critical.

EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect. Fines up to 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover. No assessed AI lab has published compliance documentation.

forwardTriggers.entity: West and Central Africa — lean season and Sudan context
forwardTriggers.trigger: West and Central Africa lean season peak. Sudan (0.0) and Nigeria (21.9, 1.9 points above Critical) face the most concentrated food-insecurity footprints. IPC Phase 5 famine-area confirmation or documented state obstruction of aid flows in Nigeria converts to a scorable downgrade.

Judicial annulment or legislative repeal of the June 8 presumption-of-legality military-force law is the primary reversal signal from the pending 18.4 Critical baseline. Further military deployments with casualties under the law reinforce the proposal. Charge adjudication for the 25 union leaders is a secondary directional indicator.

Appellate review of Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions. Reinstatement of the rescinded 30-day post-release death-reporting requirement is the first upward-direction signal within the open 06-04 proposal.

Verified Human Rights Review Board execution that demonstrably blocks or exits a harmful national-security contract — conversion trigger from the current positive-watch to a scorable upgrade within Established. Quiet reinstatement of terminated Unit 8200 access is a negative trigger.

Merits adjudication or settlement of any of the four converging sovereign enforcement actions — DOJ MA-billing criminal probe, MA AG antitrust suit, AZ AG eight-insurer cartel suit, or DOJ Antitrust MultiPlan grand-jury subpoena — is the leading corporate scoring trigger of the current cycle.

DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth. Favorable ruling triggers Functional/Established boundary crossing from 59.1; adverse ruling produces downward movement. Enacted Great American AI Act compliance documentation would be independently scorable.

Confirmation of 145+ state-attributable enforced disappearances among deportees at scale — a new harm category independently scorable beyond the priced 15.6 baseline. Judicial findings on wrongful citizen expulsion also constitute a potential trigger.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: If Microsoft's Human Rights Review Board publishes a human rights impact assessment that documents an active national-security contract and blocks its renewal, does that verified execution constitute sufficient evidence to convert the COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION confirm into a scorable ACC upgrade within the Established band — and how does the compelled origin of the board affect the weight of its subsequent independent findings?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: The COMPELLED-REMEDY ruling scores the board's creation as AB2 anchor-3 credit — but the methodology has not yet specified whether the board's verified future outputs inherit the compelled-origin discount or whether demonstrated execution restores the self-initiation premium.
